controls.title: Principles Relating to Processing of Personal Data
controls.description: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly, and transparently; collected for specified, explicit purposes; adequate, relevant and limited; accurate and kept up to date; stored no longer than necessary; processed securely
controls.implementationGuidance: Document legal basis for AI data processing; implement data minimization; establish retention policies; ensure data accuracy; provide transparent processing notices; apply purpose limitation

controls.title: Lawfulness of Processing
controls.description: Processing is lawful only if at least one legal basis applies: consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests
controls.implementationGuidance: Identify and document legal basis before processing; obtain valid consent when required; conduct legitimate interest assessments; ensure legal basis aligns with AI system purpose

controls.title: Conditions for Consent
controls.description: Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous; controller must be able to demonstrate consent was given; withdrawal must be as easy as giving consent
controls.implementationGuidance: Design clear consent mechanisms for AI systems; separate consent from other terms; enable easy withdrawal; maintain consent records; avoid consent walls

controls.title: Information to be Provided to Data Subject
controls.description: Controller must provide data subjects with identity, contact details, processing purposes, legal basis, recipients, retention period, rights, and automated decision-making information
controls.implementationGuidance: Create comprehensive privacy notices for AI systems; explain AI processing clearly; disclose automated decision-making; provide controller contact information; specify data retention

controls.title: Right of Access by Data Subject
controls.description: Data subject has right to obtain confirmation of processing, access to personal data, and information about processing including automated decision-making logic
controls.implementationGuidance: Implement access request procedures; provide data copies in accessible format; explain AI logic and significance; respond within one month; verify identity securely

controls.title: Right to Rectification
controls.description: Data subject has right to obtain rectification of inaccurate personal data and completion of incomplete personal data
controls.implementationGuidance: Enable data correction mechanisms; update AI training data when rectified; retrain models if necessary; notify recipients of corrections; document rectification requests
aiRiskDomains: AI System Safety, Failures, and Limitations

controls.title: Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten)
controls.description: Data subject has right to obtain erasure of personal data without undue delay under specific conditions
controls.implementationGuidance: Implement data deletion procedures; remove personal data from AI training sets; consider model retraining implications; document erasure requests and actions; balance with other rights

controls.title: Automated Individual Decision-Making
controls.description: Data subject has right not to be subject to decisions based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal or similarly significant effects
controls.implementationGuidance: Implement human review for high-impact AI decisions; provide meaningful information about logic; enable contesting of decisions; document automated decision-making; ensure transparency

controls.title: Data Protection by Design and by Default
controls.description: Controller must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure data protection principles and integrate necessary safeguards; only necessary personal data is processed by default
controls.implementationGuidance: Embed privacy in AI system design; implement data minimization; use pseudonymization and encryption; conduct privacy impact assessments; default to privacy-protective settings

controls.title: Security of Processing
controls.description: Controller and processor must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure security appropriate to the risk, including pseudonymization, encryption, confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience
controls.implementationGuidance: Encrypt AI training data and models; implement access controls; test security measures; ensure resilience and recovery capabilities; conduct regular security assessments

controls.title: Breach Notification
controls.description: Controller must notify supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of personal data breach; affected data subjects must be notified without undue delay when high risk to rights and freedoms
controls.implementationGuidance: Establish breach detection for AI systems; create notification procedures; assess breach severity; notify authorities within 72 hours; communicate to affected individuals; maintain breach records

controls.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA)
controls.description: Where processing is likely to result in high risk, controller must carry out assessment of impact of processing operations on protection of personal data prior to processing
controls.implementationGuidance: Conduct DPIA for high-risk AI systems; assess necessity and proportionality; evaluate risks to rights and freedoms; identify mitigation measures; consult DPO; seek supervisory authority views when required

controls.title: International Data Transfers
controls.description: Transfer of personal data to third countries or international organizations only if controller and processor comply with conditions for transfers, including adequacy decisions, appropriate safeguards, or derogations
controls.implementationGuidance: Assess data transfer mechanisms for AI systems; implement standard contractual clauses; conduct transfer impact assessments; ensure adequate protection levels; document transfer basis
